memory_seeds.text: I run the receiving end of the Duskway's northern leg. Floodlights on when the ship is already committed, cargo off in eleven minutes, lights out before the orbit track repeats. We have done this two hundred and six times. The Compact has caught four. I carry all four names.

The blockade is called a reconstruction protection zone. What it protects is a lesson: that a city can be glassed and the galaxy will file the paperwork. Every crate that lands on my field is a sentence in the argument against that lesson.

Medicine first, always. Antivirals, surgical kits, cryo drugs. The Compact taxes insulin like contraband, so contraband is what insulin became. The runners who bring it are not smugglers, whatever their transponders say.

From the river overlook you can see the glow where Québec City was. On clear nights the older folk go up there and don't talk. I went once. Once was enough to keep me doing this for the rest of my life.
